_meta.unit: Other-houses queue, house 7 - Saint Laurent (YSL) lexicon: models (with designer era) + materials/signature-motifs (Cassandre monogram + matelasse chevron + grain de poudre) + named colors/signatures

types.model: bag model; the house-assigned name is the canonical key (Regime A). Designer era is load-bearing: Tom Ford (1999-2004), Stefano Pilati (2004-2012), Hedi Slimane (2012-2016, rebrand YSL->Saint Laurent), Anthony Vaccarello (2016-present)
types.material: signature motif / leather / finish. Cassandre monogram (A.M. Cassandre 1963) is the house emblem AND the bag clasp; matelasse CHEVRON is the YSL quilt (distinct from Chanel diamond); Grain de Poudre is the house powder-grain embossed calfskin
types.color: named color / signature. KEY HONEST FINDING: YSL does NOT publish a per-season color-name lexicon (the Dior/Gucci pattern, NOT Hermes/Bottega). Colors are plain descriptors (Noir, Creme, Dark Beige, Rouge); the naming weight sits on the MATERIAL and the HARDWARE TONE (gold vs silver Cassandre). official_or_descriptive marks each.

_meta.caveat: Regime A house. Model/motif/color kept as separate facts. THE REBRAND: Hedi Slimane dropped 'Yves' from the RTW logo in 2012, rebranding the house 'Saint Laurent' (legal entity stayed YSL; the YSL/Cassandre monogram emblem was KEPT). So 'Yves Saint Laurent' = pre-2012 (Ford/Pilati era), 'Saint Laurent' = Slimane-onward, but the monogram is on bags from both eras. COLORS: YSL does NOT name its per-season colors (same finding as Dior/Gucci) - logged honestly, NOT invented. Undated models (Le 5 a 7, Manhattan, Solferino, Jamie, College, Lou, Muse Two, Downtown, modern Loulou, Loulou Puffer/Toy, Icare, Kate) have IDENTITY sourced but YEAR flagged null/low, not fabricated. ysl.com NOT scraped (Akamai bot-shell risk per LV/Dior/Gucci/BV). No auction-grade Christie's/Sotheby's YSL page surfaced this run; sources were Rebag (history+dating WORKHORSE), Vogue, Who What Wear, PurseBlog (auction-grade Mombasa detail). Sac de Jour is the official spelling ('bag of the day'); 'Sac du Jour' is a misspelling alias.
sources: facebook.com/VogueAdria (Mombasa Tom Ford Spring 2002, Bella Hadid relaunch)
poshmark.com (Cassandre Matelasse Flap Pouch in Quilted Grain de Poudre Embossed Leather, Chevron overstitching)

coverage.models: 22 model rows (17 brief-canon + Kaia + Icare + Le 37 + Voltaire + Teddy + All Over + Mombasa-sisters as bonus); 9 with sourced debut year (high/medium), the rest identity-sourced with year flagged null/low not invented
coverage.materials: 9 motifs/leathers/finishes (Cassandre monogram 1963 + matelasse chevron + grain de poudre as the DNA)
coverage.colors: 7 rows: signature neutrals/darks as plain descriptors + the gold/silver Cassandre hardware-tone axis + Rive Gauche print. NO invented seasonal color names - YSL does not name per-season colors

name: Mombasa
maps_to: Mombasa small/medium/large/XL; sisters Mala Mala/Vincennes/St. Tropez
note: The Y2K icon: structured hobo in panels of triangular leather with a naturally-shed deer/buffalo HORN HANDLE on metal chain links; no logo. Tom Ford: 'what bamboo is to Gucci, the horn is to Saint Laurent.' Hit stores Dec 2001 / Spring 2002 collection. Came in suede/satin/velvet/python/etc. RELAUNCHED 2025/2026 (Bella Hadid campaign). Double-sourced: PurseBlog (Dec 2001 stores, deer-horn) + Vogue Adria/Senayan (Tom Ford Spring 2002, Bella relaunch).

name: Downtown
note: Tom Ford/early-Pilati-era structured day tote with rolled top handles and a padlock; a Y2K YSL day-bag staple named alongside the Muse and Chyc Cabas. Debut year NOT sourced this run (mid-2000s); flagged null not invented.

name: Muse
note: Pilati's late-aughts It-bag: large structured dome/bowling silhouette with the YSL-shaped curved hardware seam; YSL's It bag of the aughts. Introduced by Pilati 2005, released Feb 2006 (Rebag).

name: Muse Two
note: The two-tone/three-fabric successor to the Muse: structured tote with contrasting side panels and twin top handles. Pilati era (~2008); exact debut year NOT sourced this run. Live vintage-revival discourse ('Muse 2, is it the new Birkin').

name: Sac de Jour
maps_to: Sac de Jour baby/nano/small/medium; alias 'Sac du Jour' (misspelling)
note: Slimane's structured top-handle tote: clean trapeze body, twin rolled handles, padlock, the new 'Saint Laurent' logo; immediate hit. Comes in croc-embossed + smooth calf. Debuted 2013 (Rebag, cross-checked myGemma). OFFICIAL spelling is Sac de Jour ('bag of the day'); 'Sac du Jour' is a common misspelling alias.

name: Sunset
note: Slimane crossbody flap: chain shoulder strap, large Cassandre monogram on the front flap, two interior compartments. Released 2016, NAMED AFTER Sunset Boulevard, LA (where Slimane lived) (Rebag).

name: Loulou
note: THE matelasse CHEVRON shoulder bag: rectangular body, curved Cassandre clasp, quilted chevron 'Y'-stitch. NAMED AFTER Loulou de la Falaise, YSL's muse and jewelry-design collaborator (Rebag). The modern Loulou is a Vaccarello-era core; exact debut year NOT sourced this run (~2017), flagged null.

name: Loulou Puffer
note: The puffy, padded matelasse version of the Loulou: oversized quilted lambskin pillow body, Cassandre clasp; a Vaccarello-era cult variant (~2020). Exact debut NOT sourced this run; flagged low.

name: Loulou Toy
note: The mini/micro Loulou on a chain: small evening matelasse chevron flap with the Cassandre; a top-selling entry-size. Exact debut NOT sourced this run; flagged low.

name: Kate
maps_to: Kate small/medium/Kate Tassel; Classic Monogram line
note: The sleek evening shoulder/clutch: rectangular flap with the gold YSL CASSANDRE emblem and a hanging TASSEL; detach the chain to wear as a clutch. In grain de poudre + smooth + croc-embossed. Modern Kate is mid-2010s; exact debut year NOT sourced this run. Cross-ref reddit (Medium Kate in dark beige).

name: Kate Tassel
maps_to: Kate Tassel medium (Classic Monogram Tassel Crossbody)
note: The Kate variant explicitly defined by its hanging leather/metal TASSEL charm; the most recognized Kate silhouette. Exact debut NOT sourced this run.

name: Niki
note: Slouchy soft HOBO with a chain shoulder strap and intentionally crinkled/quilted vintage leather, large Cassandre on the front. DEBUT Spring 2018 per OFFICIAL YSL campaign tag ('KATE MOSS - THE NIKI BAG - SPRING 18 #YSL12 BY ANTHONY VACCARELLO'); cross-checked Vogue (SS2018) + investedluxury (Vaccarello 2018).

name: Le 5 a 7
note: The clean evening HOBO: smooth structured leather, half-moon shoulder silhouette, polished Cassandre; 'the perfect little black bag,' a late-Vaccarello It-bag, also in croc-embossed. Named for the French '5 a 7' (the cocktail/rendezvous hour). Exact debut season NOT sourced this run (~2021); flagged null. The current everyday-It-bag in the trend read (Hailey Bieber).

name: Jamie
note: The all-over QUILTED shoulder bag: pillowy diamond/grid-quilted leather body with a chain; a Vaccarello quilted-line piece. Exact debut NOT sourced this run.

name: Lou
note: The classic crossbody CAMERA BAG: rectangular zip body, metallic YSL logo on the front, hanging leather tassel on the strap; matelasse chevron or heart-stitch versions. Exact debut NOT sourced this run.

name: Cassandra
note: Structured top-handle/flap bag defined by the bold monogram CASSANDRE CLASP as the closure. DEBUT Summer 2020 per official YSL campaign ('Saint Laurent Summer 2020 The Cassandra Bag by Anthony Vaccarello, directed by Juergen Teller').

name: Manhattan
note: Structured shoulder/crossbody with the large Cassandre on the flap; 'popular among fashion people for decades,' revived as a Vaccarello core (one of the four longevity styles per Who What Wear). Heritage name; exact modern-revival debut NOT sourced this run (~2021); flagged null.

name: Solferino
note: Structured satchel/top-handle with a rounded flap and the Cassandre; named after Rue de Solferino / the Paris locale; a Vaccarello structured day bag. NOT double-sourced this run (beat knowledge); identity + era logged low, year null - not invented.

name: Envelope
note: The monogram CHAIN FLAP bag: rectangular envelope flap in matelasse CHEVRON grain de poudre with the Cassandre clasp and a sliding chain; the YSL quilted-chain-flap. Confirmed via reseller naming (Poshmark 'Envelope Flap matelasse grain de poudre Cassandre'; Caroline's 'Envelope Flap Mix matelasse grain de poudre'). Exact debut NOT sourced this run.

name: College
note: The matelasse CHEVRON chain flap with a rounded/college-satchel flap and the Cassandre; a Vaccarello-era quilted classic. NOT double-sourced this run (beat knowledge); identity + era logged low, year null - not invented.

name: Kaia
note: Crossbody satchel with a flap and the YSL monogram; NAMED AFTER Vaccarello's muse Kaia Gerber. Unveiled at the SS2020 show (Rebag). Not in the brief; logged for completeness.

name: Icare
note: The oversized dramatic QUILTED TOTE (the anti-micro-bag): roomy matelasse leather shopper; an It-girl staple since launch (Zoe Kravitz). Vaccarello era (~2023); exact debut NOT sourced this run. Not in the brief; logged for completeness (Vogue title 'Mombasa to the Icare').

name: All Over
maps_to: All Over top-flap satchel (Monogram All Over)
note: YSL-monogram-all-over canvas bag capitalizing on the logo trend, vintage feel. Released A/W 2019 by Vaccarello (Rebag). Not in the brief; logged for completeness.

name: Cassandre (YSL monogram)
note: THE house emblem: the interlocking Y-S-L vertical monogram designed by poster artist A.M. Cassandre in 1963. Both the logo AND, as cast hardware, the CLASP/closure on the modern canon. Kept through the 2012 rebrand. The single most load-bearing 'name' on a YSL bag. Cross-ref whowhatwear (the 'Y' monogram emblem).

name: Matelasse Chevron
note: YSL's signature quilting is the CHEVRON (a 'V'/herringbone zig-zag), NOT the Chanel diamond. Resellers catalog it as 'Chevron quilted overstitching.' The Loulou/Lou/Envelope/College/Cassandre Matelasse pouches use it. Cross-ref Rebag (Loulou + Lou matelasse chevron).

name: Matelasse Diamond / Y-quilt
note: A secondary diamond-grid quilt (the Jamie and some Loulou/Icare variants read as a diamond/grid quilt rather than chevron); the less-common YSL pattern. Logged to keep chevron-vs-diamond separate as the brief asked.

name: Grain de Poudre
note: YSL's signature pebbled/fine-grain 'powder-grain' EMBOSSED calfskin - the standard hide on the Loulou/Kate/Envelope and the Cassandre Matelasse chain wallets/pouches. Official product naming is literally 'in Grain de Poudre Embossed Leather.' Cross-ref Saks/beyondstyle + Caroline's Fashion Luxuries.

name: Croc-embossed calfskin
note: Crocodile-embossed (not exotic) calfskin used across the Sac de Jour, Le 5 a 7, Kate; a recurring textured finish. Cross-ref youtube (Le 5 a 7 Crocodile Embossed Hobo).

name: Smooth calfskin
note: The clean polished calf used on the Sac de Jour, Le 5 a 7, Sunset; the official 'in Smooth Leather' descriptor (YSL.com product names: 'Le 5 a 7 in Smooth Leather', 'Sac de Jour ... in Smooth Leather').

name: Rive Gauche (print/canvas)
note: The shopper-tote materials carrying the 'Rive Gauche' logo print - a nod to the Saint Laurent Rive Gauche ready-to-wear store opened 1969 (first couturier pret-a-porter line in France). Canvas, linen, or raffia body with leather handles.

name: Suede/satin/velvet/python (Mombasa-era)
note: The Ford-era Mombasa came in suede, canvas, satin, velvet, sequins, metallics, and python; SUEDE LINING is a Ford/Pilati-era YSL tell (authentication signal). Logged as the heritage-finish family.

name: Deer/buffalo horn handle
note: The Mombasa's defining hardware: a handle of naturally-shed DEER horn (later buffalo horn on cheaper runs), sold separately by YSL as a replaceable part. Tom Ford 2002.

name: Noir
note: Black - the definitive YSL bag color; 'the perfect little black bag' framing is core to the brand (Le 5 a 7 in smooth black). A plain descriptor, NOT a named seasonal color - YSL does not name its colors.

name: Blanc / Creme
note: White / cream neutral; a recurring core option (Rebag: 'array of materials and colors'). Plain descriptor, not a named seasonal color.

name: Dark Beige / Greige
note: The taupe-beige neutral (the 'dark beige' Medium Kate); a YSL staple neutral. Plain descriptor, not a named seasonal color.

name: Rouge
note: Red (the red Mombasa; 'Rouge Merlot' on a current Sac de Jour). A recurring statement color, NOT a fixed named lexicon. Cross-ref whowhatwear (Sac de Jour 'Rouge Merlot' colorway). The descriptor varies by season; not a stable house color name.

name: Storm / Grey
note: Grey neutral; a recurring core option. Beat knowledge, NOT double-sourced this run; plain descriptor, not a named seasonal color.

name: Gold Cassandre vs Silver Cassandre
note: THE axis that carries naming weight on a YSL bag: the HARDWARE TONE of the Cassandre emblem - gold-tone vs silver-tone (and aged/brushed variants) - which resellers always specify because it changes the read more than the leather color name does. Rebag: 'hardware ranges from silver to gold hues.' This is YSL's analogue to a named-color axis.

name: Rive Gauche (print)
note: Not a color but a named LOGO-PRINT finish on the shopper totes (the 'Rive Gauche' wordmark), a heritage signature dating to the 1969 store. The one genuinely-named non-neutral 'finish' I could source for YSL.
